 {"id":"gt-ctr","title":"GGT vs PGT Gap Analysis Summary","description":"Summary of gaps comparing GGT (~4,500 LOC) to PGT (~27,700 LOC).

## Critical Gaps (P1) - Existing Issues
- gt-u1j.17: Polecat CLI (add, list, wake, sleep) - DETAILED
- gt-u1j.16: Rig CLI (add, list, show, remove) - DETAILED
- gt-u1j.18: Witness CLI (start, stop, status) - DETAILED
- gt-f9x.4: Doctor framework - DETAILED
- gt-f9x.5: Workspace doctor checks - DETAILED
- gt-f9x.6: Rig doctor checks - DETAILED

## Critical Gaps (P1) - New Issues
- gt-a95: Refinery background daemon mode - ENHANCED
- gt-hgk: Mail message types and threading - ENHANCED

## Significant Gaps (P2) - New Issues
- gt-d46: Mail CLI archive/purge/search - ENHANCED
- gt-e9k: Swarm preflight/postflight - ENHANCED
- gt-662: Swarm report generation - ENHANCED
- gt-69l: Hook system - ENHANCED
- gt-3yj: Agent monitoring - ENHANCED
- gt-1ky: Workspace CLI (may overlap f9x.3) - ENHANCED
- gt-9j9: Worker status reporting - ENHANCED
- gt-qao: Mayor CLI - ENHANCED
- gt-7o7: Session pre-shutdown checks - ENHANCED
- gt-c92: Batch all command - ENHANCED
- gt-lno: Swarm state persistence - ENHANCED
- gt-a9y: File locking - ENHANCED
- gt-30o: Error handling improvements - ENHANCED

## Significant Gaps (P2) - Existing Issues
- gt-kmn.12: Ephemeral rig support - DETAILED

## Lower Priority (P3) - New Issues
- gt-3fm: Mail orchestrator daemon - ENHANCED
- gt-2kz: Cleanup commands - ENHANCED
- gt-ebl: Names commands - ENHANCED
- gt-1u9: Interactive prompts - ENHANCED
- gt-8lz: Help text improvements - ENHANCED

## Closed as Duplicates
- gt-3tz → gt-u1j.17 (polecat CLI)
- gt-e1r → gt-u1j.16 (rig CLI)
- gt-86w → gt-f9x.4/5/6 (doctor)
- gt-alx → gt-kmn.12 (ephemeral rigs)

## Execution Order Recommendation
1. P1 CLI commands (existing detailed issues ready to implement)
2. gt-a95: Refinery daemon (blocks autonomous operation)
3. gt-7o7: Pre-shutdown checks (prevents data loss)
4. gt-a9y: File locking (prevents corruption)
5. gt-hgk + gt-d46: Mail improvements
6. gt-69l: Hook system (enables extensibility)
7. Remaining P2s in any order
8. P3s as time permits","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:49:09.555759-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:07:58.413016-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-cu7r","title":"Implement handoffs using pinned beads","description":"Replace the current mail-based handoff system with pinned beads.

## Current Problem

Handoff messages get closed before the successor can read them because:
1. `gt mail read` auto-acks (closes) messages
2. `bd mail inbox` only shows open messages
3. Successor sees empty inbox

## Solution

Use pinned beads for handoffs:
- One pinned bead per role: `mayor-handoff`, `<rig>-refinery-handoff`, etc.
- Predecessor updates the content before cycling
- Successor reads on startup via `gt prime`
- Never closes - always available

## Implementation

### 1. Create handoff beads on first cycle
- `bd create --title='Mayor Handoff' --type=task --status=pinned --assignee=mayor`
- Store ID in role config or use well-known naming convention

### 2. Update gt handoff command
- Instead of `bd mail send`, update the pinned handoff bead
- `bd update <handoff-id> --description='...handoff content...'`

### 3. Update gt prime
- Read the role's handoff bead
- Display content to successor

### 4. Compression/reset
- `gt rig reset` clears handoff content
- Or manual: `bd update <handoff-id> --description=''`

## Dependencies

Requires beads-6v2 (StatusPinned) to be implemented first.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T21:28:05.738035-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T01:57:17.034513-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T01:57:17.034513-08:00"}
-{"id":"gt-cxtu","title":"Implement shared beads architecture for rig","description":"Implement redirect-based shared beads to eliminate git sync overhead within a rig.

## Background
Each polecat currently has its own .beads/ directory synced via git. This burns tokens on sync operations.

## Solution
Use bd's redirect feature:
1. Create single shared .beads/ at rig root
2. Polecats get redirect files pointing to shared location
3. All agents connect to same daemon
4. SQLite WAL + daemon serialization handles concurrency

## Implementation
1. Create shared .beads/ at rig root (e.g., ~/gt/gastown/.beads/)
2. Update gt spawn to create redirect files:
   mkdir -p polecats/nux/.beads
   echo ../../.beads > polecats/nux/.beads/redirect
3. Test that all polecats connect to same daemon
4. Remove git sync from intra-rig workflow
5. Keep JSONL export for backup/cross-rig only

## Reference
beads/polecats/rictus/internal/beads/beads.go:45 - followRedirect()","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:19:53.6549-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T20:19:53.6549-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-cxx","title":"Swarm learning: Witness needs automated context cycling","description":"Furiosa hit 2% context during swarm work. Witness role needs automated detection of low context and should trigger session cycling before agents get stuck. Add to Witness responsibilities in prompts.md.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T01:21:49.67756-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T01:51:39.2553-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-16T01:51:39.2553-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-d0a","title":"Haiku-based smart stuck detection","description":"Use Haiku to analyze tmux state when signals are ambiguous.

## When to Invoke

Only as escalation tier:
1. Keepalive is stale (> 2 min)
2. Tmux shows claude is running (not idle shell)
3. Heuristics can't determine state

## Prompt

Capture last 50 lines of tmux pane, ask Haiku:
'Is this Claude agent: WORKING | STUCK | IDLE | WAITING_FOR_HUMAN?'

## Cost

~$0.001 per check. At 1 check/min worst case = $0.06/hour.
In practice, most checks avoided by keepalive signal.

## Configuration

```toml
[daemon]
smart_detection = true
smart_model = "haiku"
smart_threshold = "2m"  # only check if stale > 2min
```","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:31.287215-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:31.287215-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-d0a","depends_on_id":"gt-bfd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:46.788667-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-njr","title":"Engineer session restart protocol","description":"Implement session restart flow for when the Engineer needs to split work:

1. Engineer creates subtask(s) in Beads assigned to self
2. Engineer sends handoff mail to self (🤝 HANDOFF)
3. Engineer sends restart request to Witness
4. Witness verifies:
   - Handoff mail exists in Engineer outbox/sent
   - Subtasks filed in Beads
5. Witness restarts the Refinery session (new Engineer context)

This enables "occasionally consistent, eventually convergent" work patterns.
The Refinery continues; the Engineer gets fresh context.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:02:48.22994-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T23:07:42.05363-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-njr","depends_on_id":"gt-h5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:02:56.148564-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-nriy","title":"Test: Alpha to Beta","description":"Sibling communication test","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"message","assignee":"gastown-beta","created_at":"2025-12-20T21:44:00.731578-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T21:44:00.731578-08:00","labels":["thread:thread-d94865313b74"],"sender":"Steve Yegge","ephemeral":true}
+{"id":"gt-nti8","title":"Polecats should not push branches to remote","description":"## Current Behavior

Polecats push their branches to origin (e.g., `polecat/furiosa`), which pollutes the remote with many short-lived branches.

## Desired Behavior

Polecats should only commit locally. The Refinery handles all remote pushes:
1. Polecat works on local `polecat/<name>` branch
2. Polecat signals done (state → idle)
3. Refinery pulls from local polecat branch
4. Refinery runs tests, merges to main
5. Refinery pushes main to remote
6. If PR review needed, Refinery creates the PR

## Benefits

- Clean remote (no branch pollution)
- Clear responsibility (Refinery is the quality gate)
- Simpler cleanup (local branches deleted with worktree)
- Less noise in GitHub UI

## Trade-offs

- If polecat crashes before Refinery merges, code is lost locally
- But beads issue remains open, another polecat can redo the work
- This is acceptable for ephemeral workers

## Implementation

In polecat CLAUDE.md or landing protocol:
- Remove `git push origin HEAD` from workflow
- Replace with just `git commit` + signal done
- Refinery handles the rest","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:13:48.804954-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:13:48.804954-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-ouo","title":"gt swarm start: Does not spawn polecat sessions","description":"gt swarm start marks swarm as 'active' but doesn't start any polecat sessions.

Repro:
1. gt swarm create gastown --epic gt-hw6 --worker Toast --worker Nux
2. gt swarm start gt-hw6
3. gt session list - shows no new sessions

Expected: Polecat sessions should start for each worker.
Actual: No sessions started, workers sit idle.","status":"closed","issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:25:43.430981-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T22:31:58.849531-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T22:31:58.849531-08:00"}
